summary: Rati catapulted GSIP to v0.2.0 today, pushing nearly 2,000 lines to sync documentation with reality, refine CLI hints, and harden CI. He’s clearly not just coding; he’s meticulously engineering a production-ready machine.

Rati practically rewrote GSIP today, dropping 11,000 lines to ship a modular plugin system, YOLO-powered object detection, and hardened CI, effectively turning a niche tool into a production-ready geospatial beast.

Rati performed surgical micro-fixes on `bloom-installer` today. In just six lines, he banished "rainbow" bootloader corruption and finicky syntax errors, proving that even a tiny diff can save the startup UI.

Rati dropped a thousand-line masterclass in distro-polishing, ensuring Bloom identifies as its own beast while fixing installer quirks, syncing Rofi themes via symlink magic, and giving the branding a fresh rose-inspired glow-up.

Rati performed open-heart surgery on GSIP today, deleting over 1,800 lines to make room for multi-device support and cloud masking. He’s refactored the library into a leaner, hardware-agnostic machine.
Rati gave the GSIP docs a heavy-duty makeover today. Trading 107 old lines for 318 fresh ones in a single commit, he’s proving that writing clear manuals is a high-volume sport.

Rati went big on GSIP today, dropping over 1,100 lines to launch a new DataProvider API. He’s effectively streamlined the pipeline, allowing programmatic data to bypass intermediate files and stream straight to the GPU.
